description: Thorium Reader is a free and open-source EPUB reading application designed with accessibility as a core feature. This report assesses conformance to EPUB Reading System 3.4 specification requirements.
evaluationMethodology: This report documents conformance evaluation of Thorium Reader Desktop version 3.0.0 against EPUB Reading System 3.4 specification requirements. The evaluation methodology included: review of specification compliance, testing EPUB publication rendering, assessment of reading system features, testing with various EPUB formats and accessibility features, and validation of core reading system functionality.
conformanceSummary: Thorium Reader Desktop provides comprehensive support for EPUB Reading System 3.4 requirements. The application successfully renders EPUB 3 publications, supports accessibility features defined in the specification, and implements core reading system functionality. Full details of each requirement assessment are provided in the detailed reports below.

strengths: Comprehensive accessibility features support (page lists, navigation documents, landmark navigation)
limitations: Some advanced CSS features may have limited support
Some specialized EPUB extensions or profiles may have partial support

testingEnvironments: Windows 10+ with various EPUB test publications
macOS 10.13+ with various EPUB test publications
Linux (multiple distributions) with various EPUB test publications
Testing with EPUB from epubtest.org and W3C test suite

changelog.description: Initial conformance report for Thorium Reader Desktop 3.0.0 against EPUB Reading System 3.4

productInfo.name: Thorium Reader Desktop
productInfo.description: Thorium Reader Desktop is a free, open-source EPUB reading application available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. The application is free, with no ads and no private data leaks. It is localized in a large set of languages.

epubRS.title: Reading System Requirements
epubRS.notes: Thorium Reader supports all required and conditionally-required features
epubRS.description: Reading systems must support all required features and applicable conditionally-required features (e.g., to support images if the reading system has a viewport).

epubRS.title: Error Handling
epubRS.notes: Reading system may decline to load publications that violate authoring requirements
epubRS.description: Reading systems do not have to load EPUB publications or resources within them when they violate content authoring or processing requirements.

epubRS.title: Error Reporting
epubRS.notes: Error reporting capabilities for debugging publications
epubRS.description: Reading systems are encouraged to provide a means of accessing processing and rendering error information for debugging EPUB publications.

epubRS.title: Core Media Types
epubRS.notes: Full support for XHTML, SVG, images, audio, and CSS core media types
epubRS.description: Reading systems must support core media types including XHTML, SVG, images (GIF, JPEG, PNG), audio, and CSS if they have the corresponding capabilities.

epubRS.title: Foreign Resources
epubRS.notes: Reading system may support arbitrary set of foreign resource types
epubRS.description: Reading systems may support an arbitrary set of foreign resource types beyond core media types.

epubRS.title: Manifest Fallbacks
epubRS.notes: Processes manifest fallback chains for unsupported resources
epubRS.description: For foreign content documents not supported, reading systems must traverse the manifest fallback chain to find a supported resource or alert the user.

epubRS.title: Resource Locations - Remote Resources
epubRS.notes: May support remote resources via https scheme
epubRS.description: Reading systems should support remote resources and should limit them to https URIs to reduce network attack risks.

epubRS.title: Data URLs Security
epubRS.notes: Prevents data URLs from opening in top-level browsing contexts without user consent
epubRS.description: Reading systems must prevent data URLs from opening in top-level browsing contexts except when initiated through reading system affordances.

epubRS.title: File URLs Security
epubRS.notes: Prevents access to resources via file URLs
epubRS.description: Reading systems must prevent access to resources referenced via file URLs.

epubRS.title: XML Processing
epubRS.notes: Uses non-validating XML processor per XML specification
epubRS.description: Reading systems must use a non-validating XML processor that does not read external DTD subsets and conforms to XML Namespaces.

epubRS.title: Internationalization
epubRS.notes: Processes language and base direction attributes in publications
epubRS.description: Reading systems must process language and base direction information from xml:lang and dir attributes in publication resources.

epubRS.title: Network Access
epubRS.notes: May support network access with user notification and control
epubRS.description: Reading systems may support network access for remote resources and scripted communication. If supported, they should notify users of network activity and allow blocking access.

epubRS.title: External Links
epubRS.notes: Should obtain user consent before opening http/https links and external helper apps
epubRS.description: For links with http/https schemes, reading systems should obtain user consent before opening and open in new browser instance for privacy/security.

epubRS.title: OCF Abstract Container
epubRS.notes: Assigns unique container root URL with specific properties
epubRS.description: Reading systems must assign a container root URL to the OCF abstract container root directory with properties ensuring relative URLs don't leak outside the container.

epubRS.title: OCF ZIP Container Processing
epubRS.notes: Validates ZIP container structure and handles segmented, encrypted, or invalid containers as errors
epubRS.description: Reading systems must treat segmented, encrypted, or improperly compressed OCF ZIP containers as errors and support ZIP64 extensions.

epubRS.title: Font Obfuscation
epubRS.notes: May support deobfuscation of obfuscated fonts
epubRS.description: Reading systems should support deobfuscation of fonts using the methods defined in EPUB 3.3, though the feature is marked as outdated.

epubRS.title: Base Direction Processing
epubRS.notes: Processes dir attribute and applies Unicode Bidi Algorithm when needed
epubRS.description: Reading systems must process the dir attribute and when set to auto, apply the Unicode Bidi Algorithm starting with Rule P2.

epubRS.title: Unique Identifier
epubRS.notes: Should not depend on unique identifiers being unique to one publication
epubRS.description: Reading systems should not depend on EPUB unique identifiers being unique, as they may be reused across versions or different publications.

epubRS.title: Metadata Processing
epubRS.notes: Processes Dublin Core metadata and recognizes dc:title, dc:language, dc:creator elements
epubRS.description: Reading systems must process metadata including stripping whitespace from DC elements, recognizing dc:title as main title, and respecting creator order.

epubRS.title: Manifest Processing
epubRS.notes: Ignores unrecognized properties attributes and avoids using linked resources
epubRS.description: Reading systems must ignore properties attribute values they do not recognize and should avoid using linked resources due to limitations and risks.

epubRS.title: Spine Processing
epubRS.notes: Renders spine items in order, handles linear/non-linear items and page progression direction
epubRS.description: Reading systems must render spine items in order, recognize primary items as default reading order, and process page-progression-direction attribute.

epubRS.title: Collections Processing
epubRS.notes: Collections support is optional; must ignore unrecognized collection roles
epubRS.description: Support for EPUB collections in reading systems is optional. Reading systems must ignore collection elements with unrecognized roles.

epubRS.title: Legacy Features
epubRS.notes: Must not support legacy features in EPUB 3 content
epubRS.description: Reading systems must not support legacy features such as NCX in content that conforms to EPUB 3.x.

epubRS.title: Reflowable Layouts
epubRS.notes: Must support reflowable layouts with dynamic pagination option
epubRS.description: Reading systems must support reflowable layouts where content reflows to fit the viewport. Dynamic pagination is optional.

epubRS.title: Pre-paginated Layouts
epubRS.notes: Must support pre-paginated layouts; may support fixed-layout images
epubRS.description: Reading systems must support pre-paginated layouts, producing exactly one page per spine item. Support for image foreign resources in pre-paginated layouts is optional.

epubRS.title: Synthetic Spread Placement
epubRS.notes: Interprets page-spread-left/-right/-center properties for spread rendering
epubRS.description: Reading systems must understand and apply rendition page-spread properties to control placement of pre-paginated content in synthetic spreads.

epubRS.title: Roll Layouts
epubRS.notes: Should support roll layouts with continuous scrolling
epubRS.description: Reading systems should support roll layouts, fitting each spine item width to viewport and displaying them in continuous roll with no visible gap.

epubRS.title: Custom Rendition Properties
epubRS.notes: May support custom properties if they don't conflict with standard properties
epubRS.description: Reading systems may support custom properties provided they do not introduce expressions conflicting with standard package rendering vocabulary.

epubRS.title: XHTML Content Documents
epubRS.notes: Must process XHTML using HTML specification semantics
epubRS.description: Reading systems must process XHTML content documents using semantics defined in HTML specification unless explicitly overridden by EPUB specification.

epubRS.title: HTML Extensions - RDFa and ITS
epubRS.notes: Support for RDFa and ITS markup processing is optional
epubRS.description: Reading system support for RDFa attribute processing and ITS markup processing is optional but may enhance content semantics.

epubRS.title: HTML Deviations - MathML
epubRS.notes: Must be input-compliant MathML processor and support visual rendering
epubRS.description: Reading systems must be input-compliant MathML processors and support visual rendering of Presentation MathML if they have a viewport.

epubRS.title: HTML Deviations - Embedded SVG
epubRS.notes: Must process SVG embedded in XHTML, with CSS styling constraints
epubRS.description: Reading systems must process SVG content embedded in XHTML, respecting CSS styling rules based on reference vs. inclusion context.

epubRS.title: HTML Deviations - Form Submission
epubRS.notes: Form submission support is optional
epubRS.description: Reading system support for HTML form submission is optional and may be prevented to limit network access.

epubRS.title: SVG Content Documents
epubRS.notes: Must process SVG using SVG specification semantics with visual rendering
epubRS.description: Reading systems must process SVG content documents using SVG specification semantics and support visual rendering via CSS if they have a viewport.

epubRS.title: Fixed-Layout Documents
epubRS.notes: Must support pre-paginated and roll layout rendering; may support image foreign resources
epubRS.description: Reading systems must support rendering pre-paginated and roll layout content. Support for image foreign resources in fixed-layout spine items is optional.

epubRS.title: Initial Containing Block Dimensions
epubRS.notes: Creates ICB from viewport meta or SVG dimensions; clips content outside
epubRS.description: Reading systems must create initial containing block from viewport meta tag for XHTML or SVG dimensions and clip content positioned outside the ICB.

epubRS.title: Viewport Meta Tag Processing
epubRS.notes: Uses viewport meta only for ICB dimensions, ignores other rendering instructions
epubRS.description: Reading systems must use viewport meta declarations only for obtaining initial containing block dimensions and ignore other rendering instructions.

epubRS.title: Cascading Style Sheets
epubRS.notes: Must support CSS for visual rendering; should support CR and above modules
epubRS.description: Reading systems with a viewport must support CSS visual rendering and should support CSS modules at Candidate Recommendation status and above.

epubRS.title: Author Styling Overrides
epubRS.notes: May override author styles for ergonomics and allow user themes
epubRS.description: Reading systems may override EPUB publication styling and allow users to set default themes and style preferences for accessibility.

epubRS.title: Scripting Support
epubRS.notes: Should support container-constrained and spine-level scripting
epubRS.description: Reading systems should support scripting, including container-constrained scripting in reflowable documents and spine-level scripting in fixed-layout documents.

epubRS.title: Local Storage
epubRS.notes: May block scripts from saving persistent data via cookies or web storage
epubRS.description: Reading systems may block scripts from saving persistent data through cookies and web storage, and should provide methods for inspecting or deleting stored data.

epubRS.title: Event Model
epubRS.notes: Should follow DOM Event model; may limit script capability to disable critical functions
epubRS.description: Reading systems should follow the DOM Event model but may prevent scripts from disabling critical functionality like navigation.

epubRS.title: Scripting Security
epubRS.notes: Must assign unique origin per publication and isolation for container-constrained scripts
epubRS.description: Reading systems must assign unique origin to each publication, prevent container-constrained scripts from modifying host document DOM, and treat all scripts as potentially untrusted.

epubRS.title: Navigation Document Processing
epubRS.notes: Must provide access to toc/page-list/landmarks nav elements with proper link handling
epubRS.description: Reading systems must provide access to navigation document content including table of contents, page lists, and landmarks, relocating reading position when links are activated.

epubRS.title: Text-to-Speech Support
epubRS.notes: TTS support is optional but encouraged for accessibility
epubRS.description: Reading system support for text-to-speech playback is optional but strongly encouraged if audio playback capability exists.

epubRS.title: Media Overlays Processing
epubRS.notes: Should support media overlays for synchronized audio narration
epubRS.description: Reading systems with audio playback capability should support EPUB Media Overlays for synchronized audio playback of XHTML content.

epubRS.title: Media Overlay Loading
epubRS.notes: Must discover overlays via media-overlay attributes and support XHTML playback
epubRS.description: Reading systems must discover media overlays via manifest item media-overlay attributes and must support playback for XHTML content documents.

epubRS.title: Media Overlay Playback
epubRS.notes: Handles timing, audio rendering, TTS, and element highlighting
epubRS.description: Reading systems must handle timing/synchronization of media overlay elements, render audio/TTS, and optionally highlight elements being played.

epubRS.title: Navigation During Media Overlay Playback
epubRS.notes: Resumes playback at navigation point when user navigates
epubRS.description: When user pauses playback and navigates, reading systems must resume at the new location in the media overlay.

epubRS.title: Skippability and Escapability
epubRS.notes: Should support skipping/escaping based on semantic epub:type values
epubRS.description: Reading systems should use semantic information in epub:type attributes to offer users options for skipping content and escaping escapable structures.

epubRS.title: Structural Semantics Processing
epubRS.notes: May process epub:type attribute and associate behaviors with semantic terms
epubRS.description: Reading systems may process epub:type attribute values and associate behaviors with semantic terms from EPUB vocabulary or other vocabularies.

epubRS.title: Compact URLs Processing
epubRS.notes: May support expanding compact URLs to full URLs for vocabulary association
epubRS.description: Reading systems may process and expand compact URLs according to EPUB vocabulary association mechanisms if they support this feature.

epubRS.title: Backward Compatibility
epubRS.notes: Must attempt to process EPUB publications with version < 3.0
epubRS.description: Reading systems must attempt to process EPUB publications whose package document version is less than 3.0, according to their respective specifications.

epubRS.title: Forward Compatibility
epubRS.notes: Should attempt to process EPUB publications with version > 3.0
epubRS.description: Reading systems should attempt to process EPUB publications whose package document version is greater than 3.0 for forward compatibility.

epubRS.title: Accessibility Considerations
epubRS.notes: Should follow UAAG 2.0 and provide accessible bookshelf, UI, search, display control
epubRS.description: Reading systems should follow User Agent Accessibility Guidelines 2.0 and ensure accessible bookshelf, UI controls, full-text search, display customization, zoom, and API integration.

epubRS.title: Security and Privacy - Overview
epubRS.notes: Security risks mirror web content; developers must balance functionality with user protection
epubRS.description: EPUB publications present security risks similar to web content. Reading system developers must establish balance between features and protecting users from threats.

epubRS.title: Security and Privacy - Threat Model
epubRS.notes: Key threats: malicious scripts, content, remote resources, external links, user data collection
epubRS.description: Major threats include malicious scripts, exploit content, compromised remote resources, tracking links, unencrypted user data, and collection of reading habits.

epubRS.title: Security and Privacy - Recommendations
epubRS.notes: Should seek user consent for data collection and treat all content as insecure by default
epubRS.description: Reading systems should specify data collection, seek user consent, anonymize data, treat new content sources as insecure, and avoid XML entity attacks.

epubRS.title: epubReadingSystem Object - hasFeature Method
epubRS.notes: Must expose epubReadingSystem object with hasFeature support for scripted content
epubRS.description: Reading systems must expose the epubReadingSystem object with hasFeature method to query support for dom-manipulation, layout-changes, touch/mouse/keyboard-events, and spine-scripting.
